title: Race condition with podman crashing when ListImages API is called while an image is being deleted
body:
This appeared first time on fedora-30 update from last week, happened in cockpit-podman testing. ***It's not persistent***

The test that triggered this is running some containers from an image and then force removes all images (together with the assosiated containers). I assume there is some issue with trying to fetch the image list while something is being deleted. But this is just an dummy assumption.

/kind bug

**Description**
In cockpit-podman tests I hit once this podman crash.
I don't have installed debug symbols in the test images, so I can't provide you with a useful core dump file. Also I was not able to reproduce locally, so this makes the debugging even harder.

In the journal there in the golang stacktrace, hopefully you can figure what went wrong only from that?
Find bellow the paste from the journal.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

I was not able to find a reproducer, it's seems like a rare race condition. If you can't find from the logs what might be wrong feel free to close this issue.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.4.0
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
It's a virt-install spawned VM the host for podman.

The full journal can be found [here](https://209.132.184.41:8493/logs/pull-122-20190613-075252-4fb435c7-cockpit-fedora-30/TestApplication-testRunImage-fedora-30-127.0.0.2-2201-FAIL.log) but it will deleted after some weeks so pasting also here.

comments.body: @KKoukiou would you be willing to try upstream master? The code in question has been altered and I wondered if it is fixed already?

> @KKoukiou would you be willing to try upstream master? The code in question has been altered and I wondered if it is fixed already?

I was not able to reproduce locally, it fails only on our CI and not always. So I guess I will find out after your next release if it persists. Feel free to close, and in case it reappears  I will re-open it.
how are you removing all containers and images? are you using the prune functions or iterating over the containers and images and deleting them one by one?
@baude we bulk delete the images in that test with `podman rmi -f $(podman images -q)` and containers get deleted because of that as well.
@KKoukiou, can you try with `podman rmi -af`. That ultimately doesn't fix the issue but should be a workaround.

@vrothberg thanks for your reply. The problematic code is only in the tests, we don't run this `podman rmi` commands for deleting the images when a user performs the operations from the UI.

I have already marked the test as xfail, and I 'd rather keep it like that so that I can monitor if this issue will be fixed, instead of removing the problematic code.
@KKoukiou @vrothberg Where do we stand on this issue?

Ideally, we need a reproducer.

@KKoukiou, do you still see this issue in the tests?

@rhatdan @vrothberg I don't see this in the tests any more, but I am sure it's because we enhanced the cockpit-podman code to not greedily use the API by re-fetching every resource with each event, which is what we were doing in the past and made this race condition more possible.
Feel free to close.
